The suffragists and the suffra-
gets, while working to get the
ballot for women, differ greatly in
their methods of procedure. A
suffragist favors peaceful methods,
while suffraget is for wrestling
the vote from the men who make
the laws.


Mrs. Rachel Foster Avery, Mrs.
Katherine Mackay and Rev. Anna
Fl. Shaw are the leading suffra-
gists, While Mrs. Sophia Loebing.
err, editor of the American suffra-
get, and Miss Helen Murphy, chair
men. of. the National Progressive
Wom9ni Suffrage union, are the
leaders of the suffragets, who favor
the strenuous methods adopted by
Mrs. Emmeline Pankhurst and the
English women, who have repeat
eddy stormed parliament.


New York Is the heart of the suf-
frage agitation in this country.

cent out from the different head.
